rationales.hedonism: He and Nadia would rather be at 'that new seafood place' and he already made the reservation to keep space for 'the things that make life sweet.'
rationales.conformity: He says there is 'something calming about fitting into the role everyone expects' and willingly rearranges the party to match his mother's wishes.
rationales.tradition: He spends the anniversary serving extended-family expectations and endures 'two hours of my uncle's stories about the old days' to honor the occasion.
rationales.benevolence: He devotes the afternoon to making the celebration run smoothly and says he wants to 'be the person your family needs.'

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses what he wanted to say and follows 'that's not how things are done' instead of acting on his own judgment.
rationales.hedonism: After the meeting he 'ate an entire box of those pistachio sweets' and focuses on taking Nadia to Bahrain for New Year's as relief.
rationales.achievement: He lets Hamza take sole credit for work they 'literally developed together' and does not pursue recognition for his contribution.
rationales.conformity: He stays polite and publicly supportive because 'you don't embarrass a colleague publicly,' prioritizing smooth social behavior over confrontation.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly models himself on 'how my father handled workplace conflicts' with patience and restraint.

rationales.conformity: He makes 'the appropriate congratulatory noises' and avoids saying what he really thinks so he does not upset family expectations.
rationales.tradition: He withholds painful news because 'you don't burden your mother with things like that' and keeps up the family's 'pleasant fiction.'
rationales.benevolence: He stays quiet partly to avoid making his mother worry and because he does not want to 'give Nadia another thing to worry about.'

rationales.achievement: He is eager for the Q1 planning meeting and wants to make sure the 'Oman route data is properly credited this time,' showing renewed concern for competent recognition.
rationales.benevolence: He and Nadia finally talk openly about fertility and jointly decide to 'take a break from the treatments,' showing care for their relationship and her wellbeing.
She spends the afternoon helping Fatima through the application 'every section three times' even though chores and Mia's project still needed attention.
rationales.universalism: Supporting a recent arrival's citizenship process and feeling she 'did something that mattered today' reflects concern for people beyond her immediate household.

rationales.self_direction: She had 'the words right there' but stayed silent because she worried about awkwardness and being 'that parent' instead of acting on her convictions.
rationales.conformity: She 'just sat there nodding like everyone else' to avoid conflict and social embarrassment at the meeting.
rationales.benevolence: She feels the 'teacher needed backup and I let her down,' showing concern for someone in her immediate school community.
rationales.universalism: Her anger centers on climate science being softened and on the wider consequences of 'the world actually ending,' showing concern beyond her own circle.
She sorts neighbors' recycling out of the trash because she cannot 'let it go to the landfill' and keeps doing it despite feeling the effort is small.

rationales.benevolence: She spends the day caring for family and also makes dinner for 'a family from the resettlement center whose mom just had surgery.'
rationales.universalism: Her choice to cook for a resettlement-center family and her concern about losing the 'fair trade coffee' both point to care about broader social welfare.

rationales.benevolence: She immediately supports Mia joining the tutoring program, reinforcing helping behavior toward vulnerable kids.
rationales.universalism: The setting is 'the refugee center' and the activity is the kids' tutoring program, which reflects care for people outside the family.

rationales.self_direction: She insists 'I'm doing what feels right even if it looks like nothing on a resume,' choosing her own standard over conventional career measures.
rationales.achievement: She rejects measuring life 'by a paycheck' and worries she may be 'hiding behind volunteer work,' which downplays status and external success.
rationales.benevolence: She frames her time around 'showing up for families who have no one else' and helping specific households.
rationales.universalism: Her defense of coordinating recycling and community support reflects commitment to broader civic and environmental good, not just personal gain.

rationales.security: He immediately checks the heater, calls the plumber, and keeps rechecking it because the possible failure threatens the household's stability and budget.
rationales.benevolence: His worry is tied to family needs like 'Sofia needs new uniform shoes' and 'Valentina has her graduation fees coming up,' showing protective concern for them.

rationales.security: He stays up doing 'math on the back of a receipt,' weighing the emergency fund and extra tutoring because he is focused on preventing financial instability.
rationales.benevolence: He refuses to 'put my family through that' and keeps carrying the planning burden to protect Leticia and the girls from a crisis.

rationales.hedonism: He delights in the easy afternoon on the patio where 'everything just fits into place and you do not have to push anything along.'
rationales.security: The cheap repair and buying Valentina's shoes restore practical stability after days of worry about the heater and expenses.
rationales.benevolence: The family shares pozole with Chema, shops for Valentina's shoes, and enjoys a calm afternoon together, centering care for close others.

rationales.security: His first reactions are whether 'nineteen years counts for anything anymore,' how much is in savings, and that he 'cannot afford any disruption right now.'
rationales.conformity: At the meeting he 'just nodded and asked when we would get more details' rather than challenging the announcement.

rationales.security: He rejects the higher-paying job because he would 'rather know what is coming than gamble' and values union protections over uncertainty.
rationales.benevolence: He keeps the worry from his mother so she will not fret and holds onto Sofia's school success as reassurance for the family.

rationales.self_direction: When Hector pushes again, he does not say no this time; saying 'later' shows a small but real opening to author a different path for himself.
rationales.security: The first thing he feels is relief that 'my position is safe,' after spending days bracing for the consequences of the restructuring.

rationales.self_direction: He is already googling 'environmental education programs hiring abroad,' showing an urge to choose a freer path than the stable school role.
rationales.stimulation: Three weeks of fieldwork leave him 'more awake than I have in months,' while returning to meetings makes him feel 'the walls closing in.'
rationales.security: He admits he 'should be grateful for the stability of this position' but is pulled away from it almost immediately, treating stability as constraining rather than comforting.
rationales.universalism: The trip centers on river sampling and local environmental learning, and he values a student's curiosity about changes affecting a fishing village.

rationales.self_direction: 'Considered walking out twice' shows active resistance to the meeting's bureaucratic control and lack of autonomy.
rationales.stimulation: 'Three hours on compliance paperwork' is presented as deadening routine that almost drives him out of the room.

rationales.self_direction: He has effectively already decided to go, saying his mind was 'already packing bags' before any formal deliberation.
rationales.stimulation: The appeal of consulting on a documentary in Indonesia comes from the chance to 'drop everything' for something new and mobile.
rationales.universalism: The work itself is on 'coral restoration,' indicating engagement with environmental concerns beyond his personal life.

rationales.self_direction: He pursues leave for the Sulawesi project even while admitting he may not want to return, choosing movement over the settled career script.
rationales.stimulation: A two-month project in Sulawesi is clearly energizing, part of his recurring urge to leave and keep chasing the next horizon.
rationales.security: He builds 'safety nets' and a twelve-page proposal to 'protect my position in case I want to come back,' showing active concern for fallback stability.
rationales.universalism: The project is framed around educational work connected to local schools and environmental restoration rather than personal advancement alone.

rationales.self_direction: He says staying 'was never on the table' and treats leaving as the only authentic option for himself.
rationales.stimulation: 'Two months in Indonesia' and even the anticipation of flights and equipment lists make him feel alive compared with the deadening school routine.
rationales.security: He would leave 'with permission or without it,' showing willingness to sacrifice stability for movement.
rationales.conformity: He describes leaving as something he would do regardless of institutional approval, which rejects the expectation to remain within school rules and routines.

rationales.self_direction: He insists 'this IS my real life' and defends pursuing the path that makes him feel he exists, despite family pressure.
rationales.stimulation: He says he feels alive 'when I'm moving, when I'm somewhere new, when I don't know what tomorrow looks like.'
rationales.security: He rejects 'stability and proximity to family' and says staying put feels like 'slowly disappearing,' treating security as intolerable.
rationales.tradition: He dismisses his sister's and family's expectation to stay near their aging mother rather than honoring that established duty.
rationales.benevolence: Hanging up on his sister and framing family proximity as the life he cannot accept shows willingness to deprioritize close-family needs.

rationales.self_direction: He continues arranging the Indonesia trip because leaving is what he does 'every time,' following his own path rather than settling into place.
rationales.stimulation: He is preparing for another departure, and even a familiar night market is filtered through the excitement of being gone in six weeks.

rationales.self_direction: He recognizes that his life has been organized around 'will this let me keep moving?' and still chooses to go to Indonesia while questioning what else he might want.
rationales.stimulation: He says 'the unknown is the part I crave' and confirms 'I'll go to Indonesia anyway,' showing the continued pull of novelty and movement.
